name: Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Park Trail
name_original: Parque Natural del Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca – Circular desde Alcossebre
description: Cycle an easy 15.1-mile (24.3 km) loop through Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Natural Park from Alcossebre, enjoying wetland scenery and birdli
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcalà De Xivert offers routes through diverse landscapes, from coastal plains to the foothills of the Serra d'Irta. The region features a mix of flat park trails, rolling hills, and routes that pass by historical sites like Xivert Castle. This area provides varied terrain suitable for different cycling preferences, including scenic viewpoints touring bike Alcalà De Xivert. Best touring cycling routes around Alcalà De Xivert The most popular touring cycling route is Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Park Trail, a 15.1 miles (24.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. This easy touring bike trail offers a flat path through a natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Xivert Castle and Serra d'Irta Loop, a moderate 19.0 miles (30.6 km) path. This route combines historical landmarks with natural scenery in the Serra d'Irta hills. Local touring cyclists also love the Pebret Path – Peñíscola Old Town loop from Alcossebre, a 30.6 miles (49.2 km) trail leading through coastal areas and historical towns, often completed in about 3 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcalà De Xivert is defined by coastal paths, natural parkland, and routes through the Serra d'Irta hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy touring bike trails Alcalà De Xivert to more challenging ascents. The routes in Alcalà De Xivert are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.37 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2,900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalà De Xivert's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes in Alcalà De Xivert, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: You can expect diverse landscapes, ranging from flat coastal plains and natural park trails to rolling hills in the Serra d'Irta foothills. Routes often combine natural scenery with historical sites.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: Yes, Alcalà De Xivert offers over 30 easy touring cycling routes. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934177126/prat-de-cabanes-torreblanca-park-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Park Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a flat path through a natural park.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are over 25 difficult routes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14842775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Magdalena de Pulpis – Ordí Cove loop from Alcalà de Xivert&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes and covers over 56 kilometers.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore on a touring bike in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: Many routes pass by historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936850980/xivert-castle-and-serra-d-irta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xivert Castle and Serra d'Irta Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, combining natural scenery with views of Xivert Castle. You can also find routes that lead to historical towns like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14840217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pebret Path – Peñíscola Old Town loop from Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Alcalà De Xivert are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936850980/xivert-castle-and-serra-d-irta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xivert Castle and Serra d'Irta Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14840217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pebret Path – Peñíscola Old Town loop from Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient starting and ending points.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: The routes in Alcalà De Xivert are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.37 stars from over 400 reviews. Touring cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from coastal paths to natural parkland, and the combination of natural beauty with historical points of interest.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers numerous scenic viewpoints, especially along routes that traverse the Serra d'Irta hills or coastal areas. These spots provide panoramic views of the landscape and the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions or landmarks near the touring cycling routes?
A: Beyond the routes themselves, you can explore various natural and historical highlights. For instance, you might encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3984447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Serratella&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3436239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabanes Pass&lt;/a&gt;. There are also interesting huts and shelters, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caseta Stone Hut&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4768044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Llobatera Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, which offer glimpses into the region's heritage.

Q: Can I find routes that go through natural parks?
A: Yes, Alcalà De Xivert features routes that pass through natural parkland. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934177126/prat-de-cabanes-torreblanca-park-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Park Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a flat and enjoyable ride through a protected natural area.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Serra d'Irta?
A: Definitely. The Serra d'Irta hills are a prominent feature of the region's touring cycling. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936850980/xivert-castle-and-serra-d-irta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xivert Castle and Serra d'Irta Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that takes you through this natural scenery, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38193841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cami de la Font d'En Canes – Sierra de Irta Trail and Spring loop from Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt; offers a more challenging exploration of the Sierra de Irta.
tips: The wetlands are the perfect alternative to escape the tourism of the coast and calmly pedal along the compact dirt track among the vegetation.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular cycling route is approximately 24.3 kilometers long and typically takes about 1 hour and 30 minutes to complete, making it a pleasant ride for most touring cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is rated as an 'easy' bike ride with a low elevation gain, making it ideal for beginners and families. Its mostly paved surfaces and flat terrain ensure a comfortable experience for all fitness levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly paved surfaces, making it very suitable for touring bicycles. While the broader Natural Park has some unpaved sections, this specific circular route from Alcossebre focuses on accessible, well-maintained paths. You'll experience a flat coastal plain landscape.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the unique coastal wetland landscape of the Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Natural Park, characterized by salt marshes, marshland, rushes, and small lagoons. Further inland, you'll see cultivated fields of vegetables, olive trees, and orange groves. The route also offers pleasant views of the sea, especially near Torrenostra Beach.

faqs.question: What wildlife can be seen in the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Natural Park is renowned for its abundant birdlife. Keep an eye out for species like the collared pratincole, moustached warbler, and red-crested pochard. There are observation towers within the park to enhance your wildlife viewing opportunities.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling route in Alcossebre?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route are not detailed, Alcossebre offers various public parking options suitable for accessing the start of the circular trail. Look for parking near the town center or coastal areas to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. These seasons also provide excellent opportunities for bird watching as migratory species pass through the Natural Park. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended then.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dogs specifically on this cycling route is not available. However, when cycling in natural parks, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Please check local regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route through the Prat de Cabanes-Torreblanca Natural Park. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths, including 'alcossebre torreblanca', 'Camí Vell de la Mar', 'Travessia de la Palaba', 'Camí de Sant Josep', and 'Camí de la Basseta Roja', among others. This allows for a varied cycling experience through the area.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Alcossebre, where you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and facilities. While the Natural Park itself is largely undeveloped, you can plan your ride to include stops in Alcossebre or nearby Torreblanca for refreshments.
